features.summary: Home Depot reported Q4 2024 sales of $39.7 billion, up 14% year-over-year, with total fiscal 2024 sales of $159.5 billion, up 4.5%. Comparable sales increased 0.8% in Q4 after declining 1.8% for the full year. The company exceeded expectations with broad-based engagement across geographies, with 15 of 19 U.S. regions delivering positive comps. SRS acquisition contributed $6.4 billion in sales over seven months of ownership and is performing ahead of expectations with mid-single digit organic growth expected in 2025.
features.management_outlook: Management expects fiscal 2025 total sales growth of approximately 2.8% with comparable sales growth of about 1%, driven by continued underlying business momentum from H2 2024. They anticipate the home improvement market to remain flat to slightly positive, with no meaningful changes expected in the macroeconomic environment or interest rate environment. Adjusted diluted earnings per share are expected to decline approximately 2% compared to 2024, with operating margin guidance of 13.4% (adjusted) reflecting natural deleverage and continued strategic investments.
features.risks: The company faces continued pressure on larger remodeling projects due to the higher interest rate environment, which impacts financing-dependent home improvement spending. Potential tariff implementations could affect sourcing costs, though management believes their diversification efforts and vendor relationships position them well to navigate such challenges. Weather impacts and macroeconomic uncertainties remain headwinds, with housing turnover still at 40-year lows affecting overall home improvement demand.
features.key_drivers: Strategic investments in interconnected shopping experience are delivering results, with fastest delivery speeds in company history driving increased customer engagement and spending. Pro ecosystem investments have generated over $1 billion in incremental annualized sales across 17 markets, with all pro cohorts showing positive performance. The SRS acquisition is exceeding expectations and providing cross-selling opportunities, while new store openings (13 planned for 2025) continue outperforming expectations and contributing to market share gains.
